question: [File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: consumableUnits / Midnight.take / consumed tracking asymmetry between units and assets mode] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the asymmetry where consumed tracks units in units mode (exact, no rounding) but tracks buyerAssets or sellerAssets in assets mode (subject to mulDivDown/mulDivUp rounding), causing consumableUnits to return different values for otherwise equivalent offers that differ only in maxUnits vs maxAssets mode, under preconditions: two offers with equivalent capacity (maxUnits = N vs maxAssets = N * price / WAD) are compared, and the taker chooses the mode that maximizes extractable units, violating the invariant that equivalent offers must have equivalent consumable capacity, causing scoped impact: systematic 1-unit advantage for takers who choose assets mode over units mode? Proof idea: create equivalent offers in both modes; assert consumableUnits returns the same value for both.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: consumableUnits / TakeAmountsLib / settlementFee at TTM = 360 days boundary] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the discontinuity at TTM = 360 days where settlementFee returns settlementFeeCbp6 * CBP for TTM >= 360 days (constant) but uses interpolation for TTM < 360 days, causing a 1-second difference in TTM to produce a different fee, under preconditions: TTM is exactly 360 days (block.timestamp = maturity - 360 days), and the fee at 360 days differs from the interpolated fee at 360 days - 1 second, violating the invariant that the settlement fee must be continuous at the 360-day boundary, causing scoped impact: 1-second TTM difference causes fee jump that makes consumableUnits-derived units invalid for take? Proof idea: compute settlementFee(id, 360 days) and settlementFee(id, 360 days - 1); assert the difference is bounded by 1 CBP.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: consumableUnits / MidnightBundles / min(targetUnits - filledUnits, takes[i].units, consumableUnits)] Can an unprivileged taker cause the bundle to take fewer units than consumableUnits suggests by having takes[i].units < consumableUnits(offer), causing the bundle to use takes[i].units as the binding constraint, and then the next iteration reads a stale consumableUnits that still shows the same remaining capacity (since consumed was only incremented by takes[i].units < consumableUnits), under preconditions: takes[i].units is set to a value less than consumableUnits(offer), and the bundle iterates over the same offer twice (different takes entries with same offer), violating the invariant that the bundle must not allow the same offer to be over-filled across multiple takes entries, causing scoped impact: offer over-fill if the same offer appears multiple times in the takes array? Proof idea: create takes array with same offer twice; assert total consumed does not exceed maxUnits or maxAssets.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: consumableUnits / TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits / continuousFee * timeToMaturity overflow in take] Can an unprivileged taker cause an overflow in take's buyerPendingFeeIncrease computation (buyerCreditIncrease.mulDivDown(continuousFee * timeToMaturity, WAD)) when continuousFee = MAX_CONTINUOUS_FEE and timeToMaturity is large (up to 100 years = ~3.15e9 seconds), causing continuousFee * timeToMaturity to overflow uint256, under preconditions: market has MAX_CONTINUOUS_FEE set, maturity is 100 years in the future, and consumableUnits returns a large unit count that causes a large buyerCreditIncrease, violating the invariant that take must not overflow for any valid market parameters, causing scoped impact: DoS of take for long-maturity high-fee markets? Proof idea: set continuousFee = MAX_CONTINUOUS_FEE, maturity = block.timestamp + 100 years; assert take does not overflow.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: consumableUnits / TakeAmountsLib.sellerAssetsToUnits / buy offer path returning mulDivUp] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the fact that sellerAssetsToUnits for a buy offer (offer.buy == true) returns mulDivUp(targetSellerAssets, WAD, sellerPrice) (rounds UP units), while take for a buy offer computes sellerAssets = mulDivDown(units, sellerPrice, WAD) (rounds DOWN assets), causing sellerAssets < targetSellerAssets, meaning the offer is not fully consumed in seller-asset terms, under preconditions: offer.buy == true, offer.maxUnits == 0, offer.maxAssets > 0 (tracking buyerAssets), and the bundle uses sellerAssetsToUnits to compute units for a buy offer, violating the invariant that sellerAssetsToUnits must return units that produce sellerAssets >= targetSellerAssets in take, causing scoped impact: systematic under-delivery of seller assets when using sellerAssetsToUnits for buy offers? Proof idea: fuzz (maxAssets, tick, settlementFee) for buy offers; assert take(sellerAssetsToUnits(target)) produces sellerAssets >= target.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: consumableUnits / Midnight.consumed / group shared across markets] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the fact that consumed[maker][group] is shared across all markets (not scoped by market id), causing consumableUnits for an offer in market A to read consumed that was incremented by fills in market B (same group), under preconditions: maker has two offers in different markets sharing the same group bytes32, and a fill in market B increments consumed[maker][group], causing consumableUnits for market A to underestimate remaining capacity, violating the invariant that consumableUnits must reflect the true remaining capacity for the specific offer, causing scoped impact: systematic under-consumption of offers in market A when market B fills reduce the shared group budget? Proof idea: create two offers in different markets with same group; fill market B; assert consumableUnits(market A offer) reflects the correct remaining capacity.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: consumableUnits / TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits / tick spacing constraint] Can an unprivileged taker cause consumableUnits to return a unit count U that causes take to revert with TickNotAccessible when offer.tick % marketState.tickSpacing != 0, under preconditions: the tick spacing was reduced after the offer was created (from DEFAULT_TICK_SPACING to a finer spacing), making the offer's tick no longer accessible, and consumableUnits does not check tick spacing validity, violating the invariant that consumableUnits must only return non-zero values for offers that can be successfully taken, causing scoped impact: bundle DoS when tick spacing changes after offer creation? Proof idea: create offer at tick 4, reduce tick spacing to 2, call consumableUnits and assert it returns 0 or the bundle handles the revert gracefully.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: consumableUnits / MidnightBundles / filledBuyerAssets overflow] Can an unprivileged taker cause filledBuyerAssets to overflow uint256 when N takes each produce large resBuyerAssets values, under preconditions: N is large, each take produces resBuyerAssets near type(uint128).max (since buyerAssets is bounded by uint128 in position accounting), and the bundle accumulates them without overflow checking, violating the invariant that filledBuyerAssets must not overflow, causing scoped impact: filledBuyerAssets wraps around, causing the bundle to incorrectly report completion and transfer wrong amounts? Proof idea: fuzz N and resBuyerAssets; assert filledBuyerAssets does not overflow.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: consumableUnits / TakeAmountsLib / settlementFee at TTM = 0 vs post-maturity] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the fact that settlementFee uses TTM = 0 both at maturity (block.timestamp == maturity) and post-maturity (block.timestamp > maturity), causing the fee to be identical in both cases, but consumableUnits does not check whether the market is post-maturity, potentially returning non-zero units for a sell offer post-maturity where take would revert with CannotIncreaseDebtPostMaturity, under preconditions: market is post-maturity, offer.buy == false (sell offer), and consumableUnits returns U > 0 units, violating the invariant that consumableUnits must return 0 for sell offers post-maturity, causing scoped impact: bundle wastes gas attempting takes that will always revert post-maturity? Proof idea: warp past maturity; assert consumableUnits returns 0 for sell offers or bundle handles CannotIncreaseDebtPostMaturity revert gracefully.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: consumableUnits / TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits / id mismatch assumption] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the fact that consumableUnits assumes id matches offer.market (documented in the @dev comment) but does not verify this, causing buyerAssetsToUnits to call settlementFee(id, TTM) for a different market's id, returning a different fee than what take would use for the actual offer.market, under preconditions: the caller passes an id that does not match offer.market (e.g., a market with a different settlement fee), causing consumableUnits to compute units based on the wrong fee, violating the invariant that consumableUnits must use the same settlement fee as take, causing scoped impact: systematic fee mismatch causing take to revert or under-consume? Proof idea: call consumableUnits with mismatched id and offer.market; assert the returned units cause take to revert.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: consumableUnits / MidnightBundles.buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / targetFilledBuyerAssets == 0] Can an unprivileged taker cause the bundle to complete with filledBuyerAssets = 0 when targetFilledBuyerAssets = 0 (due to referralFeePct = WAD - 1 causing targetFilledBuyerAssets = targetBuyerAssets - referralFeeAssets = 1 - 1 = 0 via mulDivDown truncation), causing the bundle to skip all takes and transfer 0 assets to the taker, under preconditions: referralFeePct is set to WAD - 1 and targetBuyerAssets = 1, violating the invariant that the bundle must not complete with zero fills when targetBuyerAssets > 0, causing scoped impact: taker loses 1 asset to referral fee with no fills? Proof idea: set referralFeePct = WAD - 1, targetBuyerAssets = 1; assert bundle reverts or completes correctly.
